generatedSentence: Under the scorching sun, a thoughtful lone worker, long detached from the organized labor of the old kingdoms, unhooked the prototype wheels from his functional cart, a segment of his journey complete. He found a precious kernel of water, reflecting on the artifacts he'd gathered, justifying his quiet accounting amidst the uplifted trees. With windows into ancient taciturn lives, he collected a radish, a sprig of tobacco, and contemplated the animals, tucked away from the world. Further on, a derelict wreck of ramjets and grills hinted at past conflicts. He cautiously inched upstair into a structure, sensing the longest, most dangerous path ahead, where grenades might explode. He remembered the newsboy's warning about piranhas. Dressed in a flak jacket, he felt a gamer's resolve, though painkillers were a constant thought. His pants were torn, a symbol of the indolent luxury he’d foregone. An immaterial ventriloquist's voice seemed to mock his veneration for an ancient lotus, a reminder of a visited autopsy. As nightfall brought a developing density of shadow, he found himself driving into a dungeon, where a ranker, perky foe awaited. A former teacher, corrupted by international intrigue, the enemy gawks, injecting a dose of perjury into the encounter. This felt like a real-life video game, a high-stakes makeover where every penny counted. The protagonist clicked a hidden device, initiating a counter-measure, as the forum's freaked whispers confirmed an outstanding, over-the-top plan of hijinks. He launched a single coin, a neuron of hope, disrupting the enemy's concentration. The wayward projectile literally jammed the blackjack terminal, turning the tide. He saw the flicker of a jackpot, a moment of enrichment. The enemy's bladder failed in shock, his gloves dropping. The protagonist navigated the grocery-like segments of the data core, pondering the vanquished foe. The task was done, the vegetable of victory plucked from the djebel's doorway, obeying a silent, infuriating internal compass.

Further on, a derelict wreck of ramjets and grills hinted at past conflicts. He found a precious kernel of water, reflecting on the artifacts he'd gathered, justifying his quiet accounting amidst the uplifted trees. With windows into ancient taciturn lives, he collected a radish, a sprig of tobacco, and contemplated the animals, tucked away from the world.

Further on, a derelict wreck of ramjets and grills hinted at past conflicts. He cautiously inched upstair into a structure, sensing the longest, most dangerous path ahead, where grenades might explode. He remembered the newsboy's warning about piranhas. Dressed in a flak jacket, he felt a gamer's resolve, though painkillers were a constant thought. His pants were torn, a symbol of the indolent luxury he’d foregone. An immaterial ventriloquist's voice seemed to mock his veneration for an ancient lotus, a reminder of a visited autopsy.

As nightfall brought a developing density of shadow, he found himself driving into a dungeon, where a ranker, perky foe awaited. A former teacher, corrupted by international intrigue, the enemy gawks, injecting a dose of perjury into the encounter. This felt like a real-life video game, a high-stakes makeover where every penny counted. The protagonist clicked a hidden device, initiating a counter-measure, as the forum's freaked whispers confirmed an outstanding, over-the-top plan of hijinks.

He launched a single coin, a neuron of hope, disrupting the enemy's concentration. The wayward projectile literally jammed the blackjack terminal, turning the tide. He saw the flicker of a jackpot, a moment of enrichment. The enemy's bladder failed in shock, his gloves dropping. The protagonist navigated the grocery-like segments of the data core, pondering the vanquished foe. The task was done, the vegetable of victory plucked from the djebel's doorway, obeying a silent, infuriating internal compass.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone protagonist, wearing a patched-up, olive-drab flak jacket with an illuminated shoulder pad and a cybernetic eye implant. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist meticulously unhooks cyber-wheels from a salvaged cart under a harsh alien sun, ancient ruins in the background. Medium shot, slight low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist cautiously enters a dark, dilapidated futuristic structure, hand on a plasma pistol, shadowy shapes hinting at danger. Wide shot, eye-level. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a tense standoff with a shadowy, perky enemy in a glowing dungeon, a single coin glinting in the air between them. Dynamic mid-shot, slight high angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist stands victorious, looking at a broken, sparking terminal, a data core glowing behind them, with the defeated enemy slumped in the background. Full shot, eye-level.

definitions.definition: A person who performs labor for a living, especially manual labor.
A nonreproductive social insect, such as ant, bee, termite, or wasp.
A thread performing one instance of a particular task within a program.
definitions.example: This FTP client spawns a separate worker for each file to be uploaded.

definitions.definition: A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.
The light and warmth which is received from the sun.
Something like the sun in brightness or splendor.
Sunrise or sunset.
The nineteenth trump/major arcana card of the Tarot.
The thirty-first Lenormand card.
To expose to the warmth and radiation of the sun.
definitions.example: Beautiful bodies lying on the beach, sunning their bronzed limbs.

definitions.definition: To warm or dry in the sunshine.
To be exposed to the sun.
To expose the eyes to the sun as part of the Bates method.
The star that the Earth revolves around and from which it receives light and warmth.
The core, center, or essence of an object or system.
definitions.example: the kernel of an argument

definitions.definition: The central (usually edible) part of a nut, especially once the hard shell has been removed.
A single seed or grain, especially of corn or wheat.
The stone of certain fruits, such as peaches or plums.
A small mass around which other matter is concreted; a nucleus; a concretion or hard lump in the flesh.
The central part of many computer operating systems which manages the system's resources and the communication between hardware and software components.
definitions.example: The Linux kernel is open-source.

definitions.definition: The core engine of any complex software system.
A function used to define an integral transform.
definitions.example: The Dirichlet kernel convolved with a function yields its Fourier series approximation.

definitions.definition: A set of pairs of a mapping's domain which are mapped to the same value.
For a given function (especially a linear map between vector spaces), the set of elements in the domain which are mapped to zero; (formally) given f : X → Y, the set {x ∈ X : f(x) = 0}.
definitions.example: If a function is continuous then its kernel is a closed set.

definitions.definition: For a category with zero morphisms: the equalizer of a given morphism and the zero morphism which is parallel to that given morphism.
(fuzzy set theory) The set of members of a fuzzy set that are fully included (i.e., whose grade of membership is 1).
The human clitoris.
The nucleus and electrons of an atom excluding its valence electrons.
To provide an acceptable explanation for.
definitions.example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

definitions.definition: To be a good, acceptable reason for; warrant.
definitions.example: Nothing can justify your rude behaviour last night.

definitions.definition: To arrange (text) on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.
definitions.example: The text will look better justified.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.
To give reasons for one’s actions; to make an argument to prove that one is in the right.
definitions.example: She felt no need to justify herself for deciding not to invite him.

definitions.definition: To prove; to ratify; to confirm.
To show (a person) to have had a sufficient legal reason for an act that has been made the subject of a charge or accusation.
To qualify (oneself) as a surety by taking oath to the ownership of sufficient property.
A process of justification.
To remove from a hook.
To unfasten by means of hooks.
definitions.example: to unhook a bra

definitions.definition: To unfasten the bra of (its wearer).
Sound with a frequency greater than the upper limit of human hearing, which is approximately 20 kilohertz.
The use of ultrasonic waves for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.
To treat with ultrasound.
To raise something or someone to a higher physical, social, moral, intellectual, spiritual or emotional level.
(of a penalty) To aggravate; to increase.
(travel) To be accepted for carriage on a flight.
To remove (a child) from a damaging home environment by a social welfare organization.
Lifted upwards, raised.
Joyous, hopeful or inspired.
A favorable opportunity; a convenient or timely chance.
definitions.example: At this point, she seized the occasion to make her own observation.

definitions.definition: The time when something happens.
definitions.example: on this occasion, I'm going to decline your offer, but next time I might agree.

definitions.definition: An occurrence or state of affairs which causes some event or reaction; a motive or reason.
definitions.example: I had no occasion to feel offended, however.

definitions.definition: Something which causes something else; a cause.
An occurrence or incident.
A particular happening; an instance or time when something occurred.
definitions.example: I could think of two separate occasions when she had deliberately lied to me.

definitions.definition: Need; requirement, necessity.
definitions.example: I have no occasion for firearms.

definitions.definition: A special event or function.
definitions.example: Having people round for dinner was always quite an occasion at our house.

definitions.definition: A reason or excuse; a motive; a persuasion.
To cause; to produce; to induce
definitions.example: it is seen that the mental changes are occasioned by a change of polarity

definitions.definition: To arrange in working order.
To constitute in parts, each having a special function, act, office, or relation; to systematize.
(chiefly used in the past participle) To furnish with organs; to give an organic structure to; to endow with capacity for the functions of life
definitions.example: an organized being

definitions.definition: To sing in parts.
definitions.example: to organize an anthem

definitions.definition: To band together into a group or union that can bargain and act collectively; to unionize.
definitions.example: the workers decided to organize; their next task was to organize the workers at the steel mill

definitions.definition: Of a person, characterised by efficient organization.
definitions.example: Your work desk is so neat and tidy - I've never met someone so organized before!

definitions.definition: An object made or shaped by human hand or labor.
An object made or shaped by some agent or intelligence, not necessarily of direct human origin.
Something viewed as a product of human agency or conception rather than an inherent element.

A finding or structure in an experiment or investigation that is not a true feature of the object under observation, but is a result of external action, the test arrangement, or an experimental error.
definitions.example: The spot on his lung turned out to be an artifact of the X-ray process.

definitions.definition: An object, such as a tool, ornament, or weapon of archaeological or historical interest, especially such an object found at an archaeological excavation.
definitions.example: The dig produced many Roman artifacts.

definitions.definition: An appearance or structure in protoplasm due to death, the method of preparation of specimens, or the use of reagents, and not present during life.
A perceptible distortion that appears in an audio or video file or a digital image as a result of applying a lossy compression algorithm.
definitions.example: This JPEG image has been so highly compressed that it has unsightly compression artifacts, making it unsuitable for the cover of our magazine.

definitions.definition: To provide explanation.
To count.
The development and use of a system for recording and analyzing the financial transactions and financial status of an individual or a business.
A relaying of events; justification of actions.
definitions.example: He was required to give a thorough accounting of his time.

definitions.definition: An equitable remedy requiring wrongfully obtained profits to be distributed to those who deserve them.
Of or relating to accounting.
definitions.example: General accepted accounting principles

definitions.definition: A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines.
The breaking wheel, an old instrument of torture.
A person with a great deal of power or influence; a big wheel.
The lowest straight in poker: ace, 2, 3, 4, 5.
A wheelrim.
A round portion of cheese.
A Catherine wheel firework.
A rolling or revolving body; anything of a circular form; a disk; an orb.
A turn or revolution; rotation; compass.
A recurring or cyclical course of events.
definitions.example: the wheel of life

definitions.definition: A dollar.
A crown coin; a "cartwheel".
To roll along on wheels.
definitions.example: Wheel that trolley over here, would you?

definitions.definition: To transport something or someone using any wheeled mechanism, such as a wheelchair.
To ride a bicycle or tricycle.
To change direction quickly, turn, pivot, whirl, wheel around.
To cause to change direction quickly, turn.
To travel around in large circles, particularly in the air.
definitions.example: The vulture wheeled above us.

definitions.definition: To put into a rotatory motion; to cause to turn or revolve; to make or perform in a circle.
(by meronymy) An automobile or other vehicle; a set of wheels
Well-developed thigh muscles.
A length of some object.
definitions.example: a segment of rope

definitions.definition: One of the parts into which any body naturally separates or is divided; a part divided or cut off; a section; a portion.
definitions.example: a segment of an orange;   a segment of a compound or divided leaf

definitions.definition: A portion.
A portion.
A part of a broadcast program, devoted to a topic.
definitions.example: The news showed a segment on global warming.

definitions.definition: An Ethernet bus.
A region of memory or a fragment of an executable file designated to contain a particular part of a program.
(travel) A portion of an itinerary: it may be a flight or train between two cities, or a car or hotel booked in a particular city.
A bearing representing only one part of a rounded object.
To divide into segments or sections.
definitions.example: Segment the essay by topic.

definitions.definition: Demonstrating thought or careful consideration.
definitions.example: Most thoughtful people eventually solve the puzzle.

definitions.definition: Demonstrating kindness or consideration for others.
definitions.example: Preparing dinner is a thoughtful gesture for a new mother.

definitions.definition: An original form or object which is a basis for other forms or objects (particularly manufactured items), or for its generalizations and models.
An early sample or model built to test a concept or process.
definitions.example: The prototype had loose wires and rough edges, but it worked.

definitions.definition: A declaration of a function that specifies the name, return type, and parameters, but none of the body or actual code.
An instance of a category or a concept that combines its most representative attributes.
definitions.example: A robin is a prototype of a bird; a penguin is not.

definitions.definition: A type of race car, a racing sports car not based on a production car. A 4-wheeled cockpit-seating car built especially for racing on sports car circuits, that does not use the silhouette related to a consumer road car.
To create a prototype of.

A function that takes a function as its argument; More precisely: A function y=f(x) whose argument x varies in a space of (real valued, complex valued) functions and whose value belongs to a monodimensional space. An example: the definite integration of integrable real functions in a real interval.
A scalar-valued linear function on a vector space.
An object encapsulating a function pointer (or equivalent).
In good working order.
Useful; serving a purpose, fulfilling a function
definitions.example: That sculpture is not merely artistic, but also functional: it can be used as a hatrack.

definitions.definition: Only for functional purposes, notably in architecture
definitions.example: A functional construction element generally must meet higher technical but lower aesthetical requirements

definitions.definition: Having semantics defined purely in terms of mathematical functions, without side-effects.
Of a disease, such that its symptoms cannot be referred to any appreciable lesion or change of structure; opposed to organic disease, in which the organ itself is affected.

A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.
definitions.example: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned liquid, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
definitions.example: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

definitions.definition: Water in a body; an area of open water.
definitions.example: The boat was found within the territorial waters.

definitions.definition: A body of water, almost always a river.
A combination of water and other substance(s).
(in the plural or in the singular) A state of affairs; conditions; usually with an adjective indicating an adverse condition.
definitions.example: The rough waters of change will bring about the calm after the storm.

definitions.definition: A person's intuition.
definitions.example: I know he'll succeed. I feel it in my waters.

definitions.definition: Excess valuation of securities.
The limpidity and lustre of a precious stone, especially a diamond.
definitions.example: a diamond of the first water is perfectly pure and transparent

definitions.definition: A wavy, lustrous pattern or decoration such as is imparted to linen, silk, metals, etc.
Any of the carnivorous freshwater fish living in South American rivers and belonging to the subfamily Serrasalminae.
A small explosive device, designed to be thrown by hand or launched from a grenade launcher.
A pomegranate.
A charge similar to a fireball, and made of a disc-shaped bomb shell, but with only one set of flames at the top.
An unattractive girl.
A kind of aquatic plant, genus Nelumbo in the family Nelumbonaceae.
A water lily, genus Nymphaea, especially those of Egypt or India.
A legendary plant eaten by the Lotophagi of the Odyssey that caused drowsiness and euphoria.
A number of other plants bearing lotus in their scientific or common names (see Derived terms below).
An architectural motif of ancient Egyptian temples.
The quality produced by the sensation of taste or, especially, of taste and smell in combined effect.
definitions.example: The flavor of this apple pie is delicious.

definitions.definition: A substance used to produce a taste. Flavoring.
definitions.example: Flavor was added to the pudding.

definitions.definition: A variety (of taste) attributed to an object.
definitions.example: What flavor of bubble gum do you enjoy?

definitions.definition: The characteristic quality of something.
definitions.example: the flavor of an experience

definitions.definition: A kind or type.
definitions.example: Debian is one flavor of the Linux operating system.

definitions.definition: One of the six types of quarks (top, bottom, strange, charmed, up, and down) or three types of leptons (electron, muon, and tauon).
The quality produced by the sensation of smell; odour; fragrance.
definitions.example: the flavor of a rose

definitions.definition: To add flavoring to something.
To roll or throw (a ball) in the correct manner in cricket and similar games and sports.
To throw the ball (in cricket and similar games and sports).
To roll or carry smoothly on, or as on, wheels.
definitions.example: We were bowled rapidly along the road.

definitions.definition: To pelt or strike with anything rolled.
A game played by rolling a ball down an alley and trying to knock over a triangular group of ten pins; ten-pin bowling/five-pin bowling
Candlepin bowling.
Any of several similar games played indoors or outdoors.
The action of propelling the ball towards the batsman.
A particular style of walking associated with urban street culture.
(gerund) The action of the verb bowl.
Road bowling.
A marine crustacean of the subclass Cirripedia that attaches itself to submerged surfaces such as tidal rocks or the bottoms of ships.
The barnacle goose.
In electrical engineering, a change made to a product on the manufacturing floor that was not part of the original product design.
On printed circuit boards, a change such as soldering a wire in order to connect two points, or addition such as an added resistor or capacitor, subassembly or daughterboard.
A deprecated or obsolete file, image or other artifact that remains with a project even though it is no longer needed.
(in the plural) An instrument like a pair of pincers, to fix on the nose of a vicious horse while shoeing so as to make it more tractable.
(in the plural) A pair of spectacles.
A good job, or snack easily obtained.
A worldly sailor.
To connect with or attach.
To press close against something.

In scientific usage, a multicellular organism that is usually mobile, whose cells are not encased in a rigid cell wall (distinguishing it from plants and fungi) and which derives energy solely from the consumption of other organisms (distinguishing it from plants).
definitions.example: A cat is an animal, not a plant. Humans are also animals, under the scientific definition, as we are not plants.

definitions.definition: In non-scientific usage, any member of the kingdom Animalia other than a human.
In non-scientific usage, any land-living vertebrate (i.e. not fishes, insects, etc.).
A person who behaves wildly; a bestial, brutal, brutish, cruel, or inhuman person.
definitions.example: My students are animals.

definitions.definition: A person of a particular type.
definitions.example: He's a political animal.

definitions.definition: Matter, thing.
definitions.example: a whole different animal

definitions.definition: A plant of the Brassicaceae family, Raphanus sativus or Raphanus raphanistrum subsp. sativus, having an edible root.
The root of this plant used as food. Some varieties are pungent and usually eaten raw in salads, etc., while others have a milder taste and are cooked.
With a distinguishing word: some other plant of the Raphanus genus or Brassicaceae family.
definitions.example: rat-tail radish (Raphanus caudatus); wild radish (Raphanus raphanistrum)

definitions.definition: (official BIPM spelling, Australian, New Zealand, Irish, Indian and South African spelling) An SI unit of length equal to 103 metres. Symbol: km
To pull or gather up (an item of fabric).
To push into a snug position; to place somewhere safe or somewhat hidden.
definitions.example: Tuck in your shirt.  I tucked in the sheet.  He tucked the $10 bill into his shirt pocket.

definitions.definition: (often with "in" or "into") To eat; to consume.
To fit neatly.
definitions.example: The sofa tucks nicely into that corner.  Kenwood House is tucked into a corner of Hampstead Heath.

definitions.definition: To curl into a ball; to fold up and hold one's legs.
definitions.example: The diver tucked, flipped, and opened up at the last moment.

definitions.definition: To sew folds; to make a tuck or tucks in.
definitions.example: to tuck a dress

definitions.definition: To full, as cloth.
(of a drag queen, trans woman, etc.) To conceal one’s penis and testicles, as with a gaff or by fastening them down with adhesive tape.
definitions.example: Honey, have you tucked today? We don’t wanna see anything nasty down there.

definitions.definition: (when playing scales on piano keys) To keep the thumb in position while moving the rest of the hand over it to continue playing keys that are outside the thumb.
Fast growing function based on Kruskal's tree theorem.

A perennial woody plant, not exactly defined, but differentiated from a shrub by its larger size (typically over a few meters in height) or growth habit, usually having a single (or few) main axis or trunk unbranched for some distance above the ground and a head of branches and foliage.
definitions.example: Birds have a nest in a tree in the garden.

definitions.definition: Any plant that is reminiscent of the above but not classified as a tree (in any botanical sense).
definitions.example: the banana tree

definitions.definition: An object made from a tree trunk and having multiple hooks or storage platforms.
definitions.example: He had the choice of buying a scratching post or a cat tree.

definitions.definition: A device used to hold or stretch a shoe open.
definitions.example: He put a shoe tree in each of his shoes.

definitions.definition: The structural frame of a saddle.
A connected graph with no cycles or, if the graph is finite, equivalently a connected graph with n vertices and n−1 edges.
A recursive data structure in which each node has zero or more nodes as children.
A display or listing of entries or elements such that there are primary and secondary entries shown, usually linked by drawn lines or by indenting to the right.
definitions.example: We’ll show it as a tree list.

definitions.definition: Any structure or construct having branches representing divergence or possible choices.
definitions.example: family tree; skill tree

definitions.definition: The structure or wooden frame used in the construction of a saddle used in horse riding.
(in the plural) Marijuana.
A cross or gallows.
definitions.example: Tyburn tree

definitions.definition: Wood; timber.
A mass of crystals, aggregated in arborescent forms, obtained by precipitation of a metal from solution.
The fifth Lenormand card.
To chase (an animal or person) up a tree.
definitions.example: The dog treed the cat.

definitions.definition: To place in a tree.
definitions.example: Black bears can tree their cubs for protection, but grizzly bears cannot.

definitions.definition: To place upon a tree; to fit with a tree; to stretch upon a tree.
definitions.example: to tree a boot

definitions.definition: To take refuge in a tree.
Any plant of the genus Nicotiana.
Leaves of Nicotiana tabacum and some other species cultivated and harvested to make cigarettes, cigars, snuff, for smoking in pipes or for chewing.
A variety of tobacco.
definitions.example: Tobaccos from the Connecticut Valley were used for wrapping cigars.

definitions.definition: To indulge in tobacco; to smoke.
To treat with tobacco.
Upstairs
definitions.example: an upstair drawing-room

definitions.definition: To take a long position in.
Having much distance from one terminating point on an object or an area to another terminating point (usually applies to horizontal dimensions; see Usage Notes below).
definitions.example: It's a long way from the Earth to the Moon.

definitions.definition: Having great duration.
definitions.example: The pyramids of Egypt have been around for a long time.

definitions.definition: Seemingly lasting a lot of time, because it is boring or tedious or tiring.
Not short; tall.
Possessing or owning stocks, bonds, commodities or other financial instruments with the aim of benefiting of the expected rise in their value.
definitions.example: I'm long in DuPont;  I have a long position in DuPont.

definitions.definition: Of a fielding position, close to the boundary (or closer to the boundary than the equivalent short position).
(of a ball or a shot) Landing beyond the baseline, and therefore deemed to be out.
definitions.example: That forehand is long.

definitions.definition: Occurring or coming after an extended interval; distant in time; far away.
Over a great distance in space.
definitions.example: He threw the ball long.

definitions.definition: For a particular duration.
definitions.example: How long is it until the next bus arrives?

definitions.definition: For a long duration.
definitions.example: Paris has long been considered one of the most cultured cities in the world.

definitions.definition: To await, aspire, desire greatly (something to occur or to be true)
definitions.example: She longed for him to come back.

definitions.definition: To be appropriate to, to pertain or belong to.
To belong.
To destroy with an explosion.
definitions.example: The assassin exploded the car by means of a car bomb.

definitions.definition: To destroy violently or abruptly.
definitions.example: They sought to explode the myth.

definitions.definition: To create an exploded view of.
definitions.example: Explode the assembly drawing so that all the fasteners are visible.

definitions.definition: To disprove or debunk.
To blast, to blow up, to burst, to detonate, to go off.
definitions.example: The bomb explodes.

definitions.definition: To make a violent or emotional outburst.
definitions.example: She exploded when I criticised her hat.

definitions.definition: To increase suddenly.
(PHP) To break (a delimited string of text) into several smaller strings by removing the separators.
To decompress (data) that was previously imploded.
To open all doors and hatches on an automobile.

A grating; a grid of wire or a sheet of material with a pattern of holes or slots, usually used to protect something while allowing the passage of air and liquids. Typical uses: to allow air through a fan while preventing fingers or objects from passing; to allow people to talk to somebody, while preventing attack.
On a vehicle, a slotted cover as above, to protect and hide the radiator, while admitting air to cool it.
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative heat and a means of holding food under it; a broiler in US English
A cooking device comprising a source of radiative and convective heat and a means of holding food above it; a barbecue.
definitions.example: I put some peppers and mushrooms on the grill to go with dinner.

definitions.definition: Food (designed to be) cooked on a grill.
definitions.example: a packet of frozen cauliflower cheese grills

To terrify; make tremble.

The bags and other containers that hold a traveller's belongings.
The contents of such containers.
A specific bag or container holding a traveller's belongings.
Word for word; not figuratively; not as an idiom or metaphor
definitions.example: When I saw on the news that there would be no school tomorrow because of the snowstorm, I literally jumped for joy, and hit my head on the ceiling fan.

definitions.definition: (degree, contranym) Used non-literally as an intensifier for figurative statements: virtually, so to speak (often considered incorrect; see usage notes)
definitions.example: He was so surprised, he literally jumped twenty feet in the air.

definitions.definition: Used to intensify or dramatise non-figurative statements; tending towards a meaningless filler word in repeated use.
definitions.example: I had no idea, so I was literally guessing.

definitions.definition: Used as a generic downtoner: just, merely.
definitions.example: You literally put it in the microwave for five minutes and it's done.

definitions.definition: To do as ordered by (a person, institution etc), to act according to the bidding of.
To do as one is told.
To be obedient, compliant (to a given law, restriction etc.).
To make furious or mad with anger; to fill with fury.
Extremely annoying, frustrating or irritating
Any plant.
A plant raised for some edible part of it, such as the leaves, roots, fruit or flowers, but excluding any plant considered to be a fruit, grain, herb, or spice in the culinary sense.
The edible part of such a plant.
A person whose brain (or, infrequently, body) has been damaged so that they cannot interact with the surrounding environment; a person in a persistent vegetative state.
Of or relating to plants.
Of or relating to vegetables.
That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.
Excessive pride in or admiration of one's own abilities, appearance or achievements.

A dressing table used to apply makeup, preen, and coif hair. The table is normally quite low and similar to a desk, with drawers and one or more mirrors on top. Either a chair or bench is used to sit upon.
A washbasin installed into a permanently fixed storage unit, used as an item of bathroom furniture.
Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.
definitions.example: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.

definitions.definition: A common gambling card game in casinos, where the object is to get as close to 21 without going over.
A hand in the game of blackjack consisting of a face card and an ace.
The flag (i.e., a jack) traditionally flown by pirate ships; popularly thought to be a white skull and crossed bones on a black field (the Jolly Roger).

A small, flat, blunt, usually leather-covered weapon loaded with heavy material such as lead or ball bearings, intended to inflict a blow to the head that renders the victim unconscious with diminished risk of lasting cranial trauma.
Any of several species of weed of genus Bidens, such as Bidens pilosa, in the family Compositae.
A blackjack oak.
To strike with a blackjack or similar weapon.
An item of clothing other than a mitten, covering all or part of the hand and fingers, but usually allowing independent movement of the fingers.
definitions.example: I wore gloves to keep my hands warm.

definitions.definition: A baseball mitt.
The ability to catch a hit ball.
definitions.example: Frederico had a great glove, but he couldn't hit a curveball, so he never broke into the pros.

definitions.definition: A condom.
(with definite article) A challenge from one to another.
definitions.example: to throw down the glove, i.e. to offer a challenge; to take up the glove, to accept it

definitions.definition: To catch the ball in a baseball mitt.
definitions.example: He gloved the line drive for the third out.

definitions.definition: To put a glove or gloves on.
definitions.example: Maxwell gloved his hand so that he wouldn't leave fingerprints, then pulled the trigger.

definitions.definition: To touch a delivery with one's glove while the gloved hand is on the bat. Under the rules of cricket, the batsman is deemed to have hit the ball.
(usually groceries) retail foodstuffs and other household supplies.
A shop or store that sells groceries; a grocery store.
To go grocery shopping.
To furnish with groceries.
Given to wilful, perverse deviation from the expected norm; tending to stray
Obstinate, contrary and unpredictable
Not on target
A length of some object.
definitions.example: a segment of rope

definitions.definition: One of the parts into which any body naturally separates or is divided; a part divided or cut off; a section; a portion.
definitions.example: a segment of an orange;   a segment of a compound or divided leaf

definitions.definition: A portion.
A portion.
A part of a broadcast program, devoted to a topic.
definitions.example: The news showed a segment on global warming.

definitions.definition: An Ethernet bus.
A region of memory or a fragment of an executable file designated to contain a particular part of a program.
(travel) A portion of an itinerary: it may be a flight or train between two cities, or a car or hotel booked in a particular city.
A bearing representing only one part of a rounded object.
To divide into segments or sections.
definitions.example: Segment the essay by topic.

definitions.definition: A cell of the nervous system, which conducts nerve impulses; consisting of an axon and several dendrites. Neurons are connected by synapses.
A nervure of an insect's wing.
An artificial neuron (mathematical function serving as an essential unit of an artificial neural network)
